action.hypothesis: A thresholded sparse activation family may beat the current hybrid mainline by learning which latent logits should be suppressed before top-k selection rather than only reshaping logits with extra branches. Test jumprelu at K=128 with Adam as a fresh quality-first family probe.
action.summary: The latest hybrid_topk edit was a normal-runtime regression, and several rounds have now been spent on local hybrid stabilization without another gain. This round shifts back to architecture exploration with an untried family whose learned thresholds could improve selectivity at the same K=128 anchor point.
action.self_review: This is a deliberate reset to a genuinely different sparse activation mechanism after the hybrid local edits stalled. The risk is that JumpReLU may proxy poorly under the fixed-K implementation, but it is the cleanest untried family with a credible path to lower FVU.
notes_to_memory: The best current full frontier remains hybrid_topk with Adam at K=128, expansion_factor=8, FVU 0.16189537942409515.
Round 11 reopens architecture exploration after hybrid local edits stalled, using jumprelu as a new family with learned thresholding at fixed K=128.
next_hypotheses: If jumprelu shows a strong proxy signal, promote it to full before doing more hybrid tuning.
If jumprelu is flat or worse, keep hybrid_topk Adam ef=8 as mainline and then decide between a new encoder family or a training-dynamics experiment on the hybrid checkpoint.
